testcase.name: runCli > one-shot: writes the result JSON and returns exit 0
runCli > invalid JSON: returns exit 1 with an error result
runCli > a failing result returns exit 1
runCli > stream: event lines then exactly one terminal result line
runCodeTool > fails with a clear unsupported error instead of executing code
messageTranscript > encodes plain text, content blocks, and tool turns
resolveRunSessionId > prefers the platform session id, falling back to the ephemeral one
buildTurnText > keeps a resolved tool turn in the replay
agenta extension tool registration > registers one tool per public spec, schema passed through
agenta extension tool registration > is inert without the tool env (the F-005 bug shape: never delivered)
agenta extension tool registration > rejects missing required args before relaying a no-op tool call
agenta extension tool registration > does not register when specs are present but the relay dir is missing
toAcpMcpServers (http enabled, stdio disabled) > maps empty input to []
toAcpMcpServers (http enabled, stdio disabled) > throws the unsupported error for a stdio server
toAcpMcpServers (http enabled, stdio disabled) > delivers an http server, routing the resolved secret into a header
toAcpMcpServers (http enabled, stdio disabled) > delivers an http server with no secrets as an empty header list
toAcpMcpServers (http enabled, stdio disabled) > does not throw on a mix of http (delivered) and command-less stdio (skipped)
toAcpMcpServers SSRF guard (http url scheme/host) > rejects a non-https url (the secret would ride in clear text)
toAcpMcpServers SSRF guard (http url scheme/host) > rejects the cloud metadata host (169.254.169.254)
toAcpMcpServers SSRF guard (http url scheme/host) > rejects localhost and loopback / private literals
toAcpMcpServers SSRF guard (http url scheme/host) > rejects a malformed url
toAcpMcpServers SSRF guard (http url scheme/host) > allows a public https url unchanged
toAcpMcpServers SSRF guard (http url scheme/host) > allowlist opts a host out of the https + internal-host checks
otel skills + error tracing > stamps loaded skills (author + builtins) on the sandbox-agent agent span (F-029/F-036)
otel skills + error tracing > uses recognized ag.* namespaces, never ag.agent.* / ag.error.* (F-036)
otel skills + error tracing > omits the skills attributes when no skills loaded
otel skills + error tracing > stamps loaded skills on Pi's own agent span (F-029, local Pi)
otel skills + error tracing > records the error message + provider + exception on the owned agent span (F-030)
otel skills + error tracing > emits a standalone error span when the harness self-instruments (F-030, local Pi)
otel skills + error tracing > falls back to the init model provider when recordError omits one
policyFromRequest > honors the arg and the env override
decisionToReply > maps allow to ONCE (never always) so an approval grants only this call
decisionToReply > maps deny onto reject
parkedCallKey > binds name + args, order-independently
parkedCallKey > normalizes absent args to {} so a no-arg tool resumes
parkedCallKey > returns no key (fails closed) for no name or non-JSON args
PolicyResponder > auto allows and deny denies
HITLResponder > applies a stored decision (resume path) by tool-call id
HITLResponder > matches a stored decision by tool name + args when the id was not preserved (cold replay)
HITLResponder > SECURITY: approving call A does NOT auto-approve a later call B to the same tool with different args
HITLResponder > SECURITY: bare tool NAME is not a key — a stored bare-name entry never auto-approves
HITLResponder > parks when there is a human surface and no stored decision (NOT deny)
HITLResponder > headless: no decision + no human surface falls back to basePolicy (PolicyResponder parity)
extractApprovalDecisions > builds the lookup from approval tool_result blocks, keyed by id and by name+args (not bare name)
extractApprovalDecisions > ignores ordinary tool results that are not approval envelopes
extractApprovalDecisions > returns an empty lookup when there are no structured messages (headless /invoke)
extractApprovalDecisions > end-to-end: an extracted decision resumes via name+args when the approval block names the tool
extractApprovalDecisions > end-to-end: a cold replay (FRESH id) resumes the parked call by name+args
emitEvent > streaming path: flushes to the live sink and the batch log
emitEvent > one-shot path: records in the batch log only
createAcpDispatcher > disables headers/body timeouts by default so a parked HITL turn is not reaped
createAcpDispatcher > honors a positive env override for the headers and body timeout
createAcpDispatcher > falls back to disabled (0) for a non-numeric override
createAcpFetch > returns a fetch bound to the long-timeout ACP dispatcher
mapCapabilities > maps probed sandbox-agent capabilities, always enables usage, marks the source probed
mapCapabilities > falls back to no MCP for Pi and MCP for non-Pi harnesses, marked static
mapCapabilities > invariant: rejects an empty harness id
mapCapabilities > invariant: rejects a non-object probed capabilities payload
probeCapabilities > uses the daemon probe when available and reports the source
probeCapabilities > uses static fallback when probing fails
probeCapabilities > returns a complete boolean-valued flag set even when the probe omits flags
probeCapabilities > invariant: rejects a sandbox without getAgent
assertRequiredCapabilities (fail loud on tool delivery) > passes a non-Pi harness that advertises mcpTools + toolCalls
assertRequiredCapabilities (fail loud on tool delivery) > throws a specific error when the harness lacks mcpTools
assertRequiredCapabilities (fail loud on tool delivery) > throws naming both missing flags when neither is advertised
assertRequiredCapabilities (fail loud on tool delivery) > exempts Pi (tools ride its native extension, not the probed MCP flags)
assertRequiredCapabilities (fail loud on tool delivery) > is a no-op when the run carries no tools
assertRequiredCapabilities (fail loud on tool delivery) > logs the source so a failed-probe static guess is debuggable
assert (debug invariant) > throws a prefixed message when the condition is false
assert (debug invariant) > does nothing when the condition holds
buildDaemonEnv > builds the adapter path and Pi command
buildDaemonEnv > copies only known provider/auth variables, not unrelated secret-bearing env (non-managed run)
buildDaemonEnv > clears the COMPLETE provider env inventory on a managed run (clear-then-apply, rule 5)
daytonaEnvVars > combines Pi agent dir, extension env, provider secrets, and Pi command
uploadPiAuthToSandbox > uploads local Pi auth and settings when present
createCookieFetch > persists Daytona preview cookies per host
conciseError > formats provider credit failures with the right provider hint
conciseError > formats OpenAI quota failures as insufficient credit
conciseError > formats auth failures with the right provider hint
conciseError > names the resolved provider, not the harness, for a Pi+Anthropic run
conciseError > names the resolved provider for a Pi+Anthropic credit failure
conciseError > keeps the OpenAI hint when the resolved provider is openai on Pi
conciseError > falls back to the harness default when no provider is resolved
conciseError > falls back to the harness default for an unknown custom provider
conciseError > falls back to the first line
pickModel > matches exact ids first
pickModel > matches by provider suffix
pickModel > returns undefined when no model matches
allowedFromError > parses allowed values from harness errors
applyModel > uses the requested model when the harness accepts it
applyModel > retries with an allowed suffix match from the harness error
applyModel > falls back to harness default when no match exists
signSessionMountCredentials > maps the API snake_case response to camelCase credentials
signSessionMountCredentials > returns null on 503 (mounts disabled) without throwing
signSessionMountCredentials > returns null when credentials are incomplete
signSessionMountCredentials > returns null when fetch throws (network error)
mountStorage > builds the geesefs command with creds in env, not argv
mountStorage > omits --endpoint for AWS S3 (empty endpoint)
mountStorage > is a no-op when already mounted (idempotent)
mountStorage > returns false (does not throw) when geesefs fails
unmountStorage > runs fusermount -u via the injected runner
unmountStorage > swallows unmount errors (data lives in the store)
discoverTunnelEndpoint (remote) > prefers the https public_url from the ngrok agent API
discoverTunnelEndpoint (remote) > returns null when no tunnel is up
discoverTunnelEndpoint (remote) > returns null when the agent API is unreachable
mountStorageRemote > execs geesefs IN the sandbox with the tunnel endpoint and creds in env
mountStorageRemote > returns false on a non-zero geesefs exit (no throw)
runSandboxAgent orchestration > returns a successful one-shot result and cleans up acquired resources
runSandboxAgent orchestration > keeps terminal events empty on the streaming path
runSandboxAgent orchestration > surfaces permission requests and answers them through the responder
runSandboxAgent orchestration > starts and stops the tool relay only when executable tools are present

failure.message:
Expected values to be strictly deep-equal: + actual - expected ... Skipped lines
[ 'local-relay-host', '/tmp/agenta-fake-cwd/.agenta-tools', [ { ... 'auto', + undefined, + { + onClientTool: [AsyncFunction: onClientTool], + onPark: [Function: onPark] + } ]

testcase.failure:
AssertionError: Expected values to be strictly deep-equal: + actual - expected ... Skipped lines
[ 'local-relay-host', '/tmp/agenta-fake-cwd/.agenta-tools', [ { ... 'auto', + undefined, + { + onClientTool: [AsyncFunction: onClientTool], + onPark: [Function: onPark] + } ]
- Expected + Received
@@ -7,6 +7,11 @@ "name": "server_tool", }, ], undefined, "auto", + undefined, + { + "onClientTool": [Function onClientTool], + "onPark": [Function onPark], + }, ]
❯ tests/unit/sandbox-agent-orchestration.test.ts:357:12

testcase.name: runSandboxAgent orchestration > delivers a non-Pi run's gateway tools over the internal HTTP MCP channel
runSandboxAgent orchestration > still refuses a run carrying a USER stdio MCP server (user gate untouched)
runSandboxAgent orchestration > fails loud when a non-Pi harness probes mcpTools:false but the run carries tools
runSandboxAgent orchestration > does not gate tool delivery for a Pi run even when mcpTools is false
runSandboxAgent orchestration > flushes a partial trace and cleans up on prompt errors
runSandboxAgent orchestration > passes the sandbox permission through to buildSandboxProvider
runSandboxAgent orchestration > passes cancellation signals into SandboxAgent.start
runSandboxAgent orchestration > clears inherited provider env on a managed run and applies ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL for claude
runSandboxAgent orchestration > does not set ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH for a non-claude (pi) run
runSandboxAgent orchestration > sets Claude Bedrock env and strict selected model pass-through
runSandboxAgent orchestration > sets Claude Vertex env and selected model pass-through
runSandboxAgent orchestration > does not clear provider env or set a base url on a runtime_provided run
runSandboxAgent default HITL responder wiring > headless (/invoke: no sessionId, no decisions) auto-allows — no regression
runSandboxAgent default HITL responder wiring > human surface (/messages: sessionId set) with no decision PARKS the tool, no harness reply (F-024)
runSandboxAgent default HITL responder wiring > park ENDS the turn even when the prompt hangs: terminal stopReason 'paused', no harness reply (F-040)
runSandboxAgent default HITL responder wiring > park terminates even if the managed cancel rejects (local park signal still ends the turn)
runSandboxAgent default HITL responder wiring > human surface with a stored approval resumes the tool (once)
attachPermissionResponder > emits an interaction_request and answers the ACP permission
attachPermissionResponder > parks: emits the interaction_request but sends NO harness reply (F-024 regression)
attachPermissionResponder > does not respond when the ACP request has no id
buildPiExtensionEnv > exposes tracing, usage, and public tool metadata only
buildPiExtensionEnv > omits trace and tool env when tracing and relay are disabled
buildPiExtensionEnv > accepts snake_case tool schemas from older Python wire payloads
buildPiExtensionEnv > carries the loaded skill names under tracing (F-029)
buildPiExtensionEnv > omits the loaded skills env when there are none or tracing is off
writeSystemPromptLocal > writes replacement and append prompt files
prepareLocalAgentDir > seeds auth/settings and installs materialized skills into a throwaway dir
sandbox uploads > recursively uploads files into sandbox fs
sandbox uploads > uploads each materialized skill under the Pi skills directory
findSwallowedPiError > returns the last assistant errorMessage for a matching cwd
findSwallowedPiError > returns undefined for a successful turn
findSwallowedPiError > does not surface an error cleared by a later successful turn
findSwallowedPiError > ignores transcripts for a different cwd
findSwallowedPiError > returns undefined when the sessions dir is absent
daytonaNetworkFields > blocks all egress for network:off
daytonaNetworkFields > renders a non-empty allowlist as a comma-separated CIDR string
daytonaNetworkFields > blocks all egress for an empty allowlist (allow zero ranges == allow nothing)
daytonaNetworkFields > leaves the sandbox default-open for network:on
daytonaNetworkFields > leaves the sandbox default-open when no policy is set
daytonaAutoStopMinutes (leak backstop) > uses the env value when it is a positive integer
daytonaAutoStopMinutes (leak backstop) > floors a fractional env value to whole minutes
daytonaAutoStopMinutes (leak backstop) > falls back to the default when the env is unset
daytonaAutoStopMinutes (leak backstop) > falls back to the default for a non-numeric env value
daytonaAutoStopMinutes (leak backstop) > clamps 0 to the default so auto-stop is never re-disabled (the leak)
daytonaAutoStopMinutes (leak backstop) > clamps a negative env value to the default
daytonaAutoStopMinutes (leak backstop) > the default is a positive backstop (auto-stop stays ON)
buildDaytonaCreate (leak backstop on the create object) > carries a positive auto-stop interval + ephemeral by default (self-reaps a leak)
buildDaytonaCreate (leak backstop on the create object) > carries the env-configured auto-stop interval
buildRunPlan > returns the current no-prompt error without creating a cwd
buildRunPlan > normalizes an Agenta/Pi local run and filters executable tools
buildRunPlan > keeps the relay enabled for client-only Pi tools
buildRunPlan > carries the sandbox permission onto the plan and leaves an unrestricted run alone
buildRunPlan > treats an absent sandbox permission as unrestricted
buildRunPlan > errors on a restricted-network run on the local sandbox under strict
buildRunPlan > errors on a restricted-network run on the local sandbox even under best_effort
buildRunPlan > allows an unrestricted (network: on) run on the local sandbox
buildRunPlan > errors when filesystem is specified (not implemented on any backend)
buildRunPlan > rejects a strict restricted-network Daytona run with a runner-host tool
buildRunPlan > treats an OMITTED enforcement as strict (LOW-6: wire-schema default is strict)
buildRunPlan > lets best_effort opt out of the runner-host-tool guard (LOW-6 contrast)
buildRunPlan > errors on any run carrying a stdio MCP server (MCP disabled)
buildRunPlan > errors on a stdio MCP server on the local sandbox too (non-Pi harness)
buildRunPlan > errors LOUD on a Pi run carrying a user STDIO MCP server (F-032, Pi-specific)
buildRunPlan > errors LOUD on a Pi run carrying a user HTTP MCP server (F-032 silent-drop fix)
buildRunPlan > allows a non-Pi (claude) run with a user HTTP MCP server (Pi gate is Pi-only)
buildRunPlan > errors on any run carrying a code tool (code execution removed, fail loud)
buildRunPlan > allows a run with a non-code (callback) tool
buildRunPlan > allows a strict restricted-network Daytona run with only a remote MCP server
buildRunPlan > allows a best_effort restricted-network Daytona run with a runner-host tool
buildRunPlan > allows a strict Daytona run with a clean network boundary (no host tools)
buildRunPlan > materializes skills for Claude so workspace preparation can write .claude/skills
buildRunPlan > stays quiet for a Claude harness with no skills
buildRunPlan > normalizes a Daytona Claude run without Pi-only state
shouldUploadOwnLogin > never uploads when the connection resolved a real key (credentialMode 'env')
shouldUploadOwnLogin > uploads for runtime_provided (the harness authenticates with its own login)
shouldUploadOwnLogin > never uploads for credentialMode 'none' (no credential asserted)
shouldUploadOwnLogin > falls back to the hasApiKey heuristic for an un-migrated caller (no credentialMode)
readRunUsage > reads local Pi usage writeback
readRunUsage > reads Daytona Pi usage writeback through the sandbox fs API
mergePromptAndStreamUsage > combines prompt token split with stream cost
mergePromptAndStreamUsage > returns undefined when no usage was reported
resolveRunUsage > prefers Pi usage writeback over prompt/stream fallback
prepareWorkspace > prepares and cleans a local cwd
prepareWorkspace > writes a nested harnessFiles entry (.claude/settings.json) for a local run
prepareWorkspace > writes no harness file for a plan with no harnessFiles
prepareWorkspace > prepares a Daytona cwd through the sandbox fs API
prepareWorkspace > writes a nested harnessFiles entry on Daytona via the fs API
prepareWorkspace > writes no harness file on Daytona for a plan with no harnessFiles
prepareWorkspace > writes Claude skills into the project-local .claude/skills tree for a local run
prepareWorkspace > uploads Claude skills into the project-local .claude/skills tree on Daytona
createAgentServer > GET /health returns runner identity
createAgentServer > POST /run returns the engine result (200)
createAgentServer > POST /run with invalid JSON returns 400
createAgentServer > a failing result returns 500
createAgentServer > POST /run is accepted with no token configured (default-off, network isolation only)
createAgentServer > POST /run without the token returns 401 when a token is configured
createAgentServer > POST /run with a wrong token returns 401
createAgentServer > POST /run accepts the matching token via Authorization: Bearer
createAgentServer > POST /run accepts the matching token via X-Agenta-Runner-Token
createAgentServer > GET /health is reachable without the token even when one is configured
createAgentServer > NDJSON stream: events first, then exactly one terminal result with no echoed events
registerShutdownHandler (sandbox-leak backstop on docker stop) > registers a listener for each shutdown signal
registerShutdownHandler (sandbox-leak backstop on docker stop) > runs cleanup then exits when a signal fires
registerShutdownHandler (sandbox-leak backstop on docker stop) > still exits when cleanup rejects (cleanup must never block shutdown)
registerShutdownHandler (sandbox-leak backstop on docker stop) > cleans up only once even if the signal fires repeatedly
startAliveWatchdog > sends an immediate heartbeat on start carrying turn_id and is_running=true
startAliveWatchdog > uses a stable replica_id across turns (distinct from turn_id)
startAliveWatchdog > release() sends a final heartbeat with is_running=false and status=ended
startAliveWatchdog > swallows heartbeat failures — never throws
createInteraction > POSTs to /sessions/interactions with correct body shape
createInteraction > retries on failure then gives up without throwing
createInteraction > succeeds after partial failures
createInteraction > does not throw when auth returns empty string
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > (a) gateway tools + no user MCP -> internal channel present, no throw
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > (b) user stdio MCP + no gateway tools -> refused (user gate untouched)
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > (c) gateway tools + user http MCP -> BOTH delivered; user stdio still refused
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > Pi gets [] (native delivery, no MCP channel) even with gateway tools
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > a non-MCP harness gets [] (capability gate), no internal server started
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > the internal channel advertisement carries no credential (server-side invariant)
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > (Daytona) gateway tools -> NO internal loopback advertisement (file relay delivers them)
buildSessionMcpServers layering (do-not-merge regression guard) > (Daytona) a user http MCP is STILL delivered (remote url, not a runner loopback)
buildPersistingEmitter > persists a plain message event and forwards to the live emitter
buildPersistingEmitter > coalesces message_start/delta/end into a single persisted message
buildPersistingEmitter > persists done and tool events as-is
buildPersistingEmitter > event_index increments monotonically across events
drainPersist > resolves immediately when no events are pending
drainPersist > waits for all queued events before resolving
session Redis contract: TTLs > alive TTL matches golden
session Redis contract: TTLs > running TTL matches golden
session Redis contract: TTLs > attached TTL matches golden
session Redis contract: TTLs > owner TTL matches golden
session Redis contract: TTLs > heartbeat interval matches golden
session Redis contract: TTLs > heartbeat write threshold matches golden
session Redis contract: key builders > aliveKey matches golden
session Redis contract: key builders > runningKey matches golden
session Redis contract: key builders > attachedKey matches golden
session Redis contract: key builders > ownerKey matches golden
session Redis contract: key builders > displacedChannel matches golden
session Redis contract: displacement payload > reason constant matches golden
session Redis contract: displacement payload > makeDisplacementPayload matches golden shape
session Redis contract: Lua script > RELEASE_IF_OWNER_LUA matches golden exactly
session Redis contract: caps and validation > concurrency cap matches golden
session Redis contract: caps and validation > session id max length matches golden
session Redis contract: caps and validation > validateSessionId accepts valid ids
session Redis contract: caps and validation > validateSessionId rejects invalid ids
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > writes SKILL.md with composed frontmatter and the body
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > emits disable-model-invocation in the frontmatter only when set
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > uses the hyphenated disable-model-invocation key, never the camelCase/snake_case spellings
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > lays bundled files at their relative paths
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > does NOT chmod +x an executable file when policy is deny (default)
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > does NOT chmod +x when the skill disallows executable files, even with an allow policy
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > chmod +x ONLY when the skill allows AND the policy allows
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > skips an unsafe file path (absolute / parent escape) but keeps the skill
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > materializes multiple skills into separate dirs under one root
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > treats empty / undefined input as a no-op
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > rejects a skill whose name would traverse out of the root (untrusted wire)
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > rejects a bundled file that targets SKILL.md (would clobber the composed frontmatter)
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > escapes YAML-breaking characters in the description scalar
resolveSkillDirs (materializer) > cleanup() removes the per-run temp root
isBannerLine > matches every banner-line variant the user saw
isBannerLine > does not match genuine answer lines
stripStartupBanner (coalesced / finish path) > strips the raw markdown banner the user saw
stripStartupBanner (coalesced / finish path) > strips the rendered banner the user saw
stripStartupBanner (coalesced / finish path) > strips a multi-line answer's banner but keeps the whole answer
stripStartupBanner (coalesced / finish path) > leaves a clean answer untouched
stripStartupBanner (coalesced / finish path) > returns empty when the text is only the banner
splitLeadingBanner (streaming path) > holds emission while only banner-or-blank has arrived
splitLeadingBanner (streaming path) > does not judge an incomplete trailing line
splitLeadingBanner (streaming path) > settles and returns the body once a real line completes
splitLeadingBanner (streaming path) > holds the first real line until its newline arrives (boundary safety)
splitLeadingBanner (streaming path) > settles immediately for a banner-free stream
createSandboxAgentOtel streaming suppresses the banner > never streams banner deltas, only the real answer
createSandboxAgentOtel streaming suppresses the banner > strips the banner on finish when the stream had no clean body line
createSandboxAgentOtel state machine > scenario 1: streaming (emit set) yields pure deltas and balanced lifecycle
createSandboxAgentOtel state machine > scenario 2: one-shot (no emit) coalesces text/thought and keeps structured events
createSandboxAgentOtel state machine > scenario 3: span-less mode still records ACP events and final usage
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > starts one internal http server for an executable callback run, with NO credentials
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > starts the server for a code run too (executable)
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > returns [] for empty specs (no-tools path untouched)
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > returns [] for client-only specs (nothing executable goes through the channel)
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > starts the server when an executable spec sits beside a client spec
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > the served MCP endpoint > answers initialize / tools/list (public spec only, client filtered)
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > the served MCP endpoint > routes tools/call through the relay dir (server-side execution)
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > the served MCP endpoint > returns an MCP error for an unknown tool
buildToolMcpServers (internal gateway-tool channel) > the served MCP endpoint > accepts a notification with no response (202)
assembleBody > deep-sets the model args at args_into
assembleBody > merges the model args at the root when args_into is absent
assembleBody > overlays the reference invoke: args at data.inputs, fixed revision at the root
assembleBody > lets the server-fixed body win over a colliding model arg (no retargeting)
assembleBody > fixed body wins on a nested collision, too
assembleBody > drops non-object args when there is no args_into (nowhere safe at the root)
assembleBody > is prototype-pollution-safe via args_into
assembleBody > is prototype-pollution-safe via a body key
assembleBody context binding > binds a $ctx value from the run context, deep-set at the mapped path
assembleBody context binding > fails closed when a run-context key is missing
assembleBody context binding > fails closed when a colliding model arg cannot be bound from context
assembleBody context binding > fails closed when a colliding static body field cannot be bound from context
assembleBody context binding > fails closed when run context is absent
assembleBody context binding > lets a bound field win over a colliding model arg (the model cannot override it)
assembleBody context binding > lets a bound field win over a static body field (context is filled last)
assembleBody context binding > fails closed on a malformed context token (one without the $ctx. prefix)
assembleBody context binding > is prototype-pollution-safe on the bound body path
resolveCtxToken > navigates a dotted path against the run context
resolveCtxToken > returns undefined for a missing key, a malformed token, or no run context
resolveCtxToken > rejects unsafe / inherited segments in the token (prototype-safe at the source)
deepSet / deepMerge > deepSet rejects an empty path segment
deepSet / deepMerge > deepSet replaces a non-object intermediate
deepSet / deepMerge > deepMerge has the overlay win and does not mutate inputs
deepSet / deepMerge > deepDelete removes a nested leaf and no-ops a missing parent, proto-safely
directCallUrl > joins the callback origin with the /api path
directCallUrl > preserves a non-default port from the callback origin
directCallUrl > keeps a query string
directCallUrl > allows DELETE and substitutes scalar path parameters
directCallUrl > rejects a missing path parameter
directCallUrl > rejects a non-scalar path parameter
directCallUrl > rejects a disallowed method
directCallUrl > accepts a path on a non-/api mount (OSS self-host at the origin root)
directCallUrl > rejects a same-origin path outside the callback's mount
directCallUrl > rejects a percent-encoded traversal that normalizes out of the mount
directCallUrl > rejects an absolute URL as the path
directCallUrl > rejects a protocol-relative path
directCallUrl > rejects a literal traversal path
directCallUrl > rejects a callback endpoint with no usable origin
pathParamNames > extracts the {name} tokens from a path
pathParamNames > returns an empty list for a path with no params or a non-string
startToolRelay direct branch (host makes the call for the sandbox) > makes the direct call host-side from a relayed name + args
startToolRelay direct branch (host makes the call for the sandbox) > binds run context into the relayed direct call, server-side (model never sets it)
startToolRelay direct branch (host makes the call for the sandbox) > strips substituted path params out of the POST body
startToolRelay direct branch (host makes the call for the sandbox) > surfaces the SSRF-guard rejection as a relay error
startToolRelay direct branch (host makes the call for the sandbox) > returns a generic error on a non-2xx (no internal URL or upstream body leaks)
testcase.system-err: direct tool call POST https://agenta.example/api/workflows/invoke returned HTTP 500: INTERNAL stack trace + secret detail

testcase.name: runResolvedTool > fails code specs with a clear unsupported error
runResolvedTool > throws for a client spec (never executed in-sandbox)
runResolvedTool > relays a client spec when the Pi extension has a runner relay
runResolvedTool > rejects missing required args before any relay or callback execution
relayToolCall (Daytona file relay) > returns the relayed text when the response is ok
relayToolCall (Daytona file relay) > reports the tool name on an empty relay error (regression for the callRef bug)
resolvePermission > permission=allow policy=auto -> allow
resolvePermission > permission=allow policy=deny -> allow
resolvePermission > permission=deny policy=auto -> deny
resolvePermission > permission=deny policy=deny -> deny
resolvePermission > permission=ask policy=auto -> allow
resolvePermission > permission=ask policy=deny -> deny
resolvePermission > permission=unset policy=auto -> allow
resolvePermission > permission=unset policy=deny -> deny
resolvePermission > permission=bogus policy=auto -> allow
resolvePermission > permission=bogus policy=deny -> deny
startToolRelay permission enforcement > refuses a deny spec without executing its code
startToolRelay permission enforcement > returns unsupported for an allow code spec
startToolRelay permission enforcement > rejects missing required args before executing a relayed tool
startToolRelay permission enforcement > refuses an unset spec when the headless policy is deny
startToolRelay permission enforcement > parks a browser-fulfilled client tool without writing a relay response
startToolRelay permission enforcement > still parks a browser-fulfilled client tool under deny permission policy
startToolRelay permission enforcement > writes stored client-tool output when the browser already fulfilled it
wire contract: requests (vs Python golden) > run_request.pi_core.json: every top-level key is known to AgentRunRequest
wire contract: requests (vs Python golden) > run_request.claude.json: every top-level key is known to AgentRunRequest
wire contract: requests (vs Python golden) > pi request: shape, tool axes, and the runner helpers
wire contract: requests (vs Python golden) > claude request: gates tool use, no prompt overrides, null session id
wire contract: results (vs Python golden) > ok result: shape, events, and camelCase capabilities
wire contract: results (vs Python golden) > error result: ok=false carries the error message
wire contract: results (vs Python golden) > minimal ok result: bare success is valid
